name: B.O.N.D. Community
description_short: Federal credit union in Atlanta with $45M in assets, 3,219 members, and 2 branches offering checking, savings, and loan services since 1972.
description_long:
B.O.N.D. Community Federal Credit Union is a community-based federal credit union headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, that has served its membership for over 50 years since its establishment in 1972. With a charter number of 20974 and classification as a federal credit union, it operates as a member-owned financial cooperative serving the Atlanta community. The institution currently maintains 2 branch locations and manages approximately $45.06 million in total assets with a membership base of 3,219 people.

The credit union offers a range of traditional banking services including checking and savings accounts, ATM/debit card programs, and various loan products. Their loan portfolio reflects a balanced approach with $20.66 million in real estate loans, $14.07 million in other loans, and $1.83 million in unsecured loans. The institution generates income through loan originations, investments, and other financial services, maintaining operational stability with $2.56 million in cash and equivalents.

B.O.N.D. Community's membership classification as a community credit union (neither low-income designated nor restricted to specific employers) allows it to serve the broader Atlanta community rather than a single employer or demographic segment. The credit union maintains relatively conservative financial practices, with no corporate credit union affiliation and no FHLB membership, operating primarily on traditional deposits and loan revenue. Leadership includes CEO Craig McDonald and Chairperson William Carmichael, with 13 full and part-time employees managing day-to-day operations.

As a smaller community credit union, B.O.N.D. Community offers localized service and member-centric governance typical of credit unions, though with limited branch presence compared to larger financial institutions. The institution demonstrated net income of $269,871 in its most recent reporting period and maintains solid equity capital of $5.14 million. For Atlanta-area residents seeking community banking relationships, this credit union provides traditional services, though prospective members should verify specific product offerings and rates directly, as detailed service specifications are not fully outlined in available documentation. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Member deposit accounts with NCUSIF insurance protection
pros: Federal credit union with 54-year operational history and proven stability since 1972
Member-owned structure means profits return to members as lower fees and better rates
Located in Atlanta with 2 physical branch locations for in-person service at 433 Moreland Ave NE and additional branch
NCUSIF deposit insurance protection ($408,856 on reserve) safeguards member funds
Balanced loan portfolio ($36.56M total) demonstrates lending capacity for mortgages, personal loans, and real estate financing
Modest size ($45M assets, 3,219 members) allows personalized member service and community relationships
ATM/debit card programs and low-cost wire transfers provide convenient transaction options
cons: Limited branch presence (only 2 locations) restricts accessibility compared to larger regional or national banks
Smaller asset base ($45M) means potentially narrower product range and less competitive rates on some products
Not FHLB member, which may limit access to certain wholesale funding and emergency liquidity resources
No low-income designation may mean fewer specialized programs for underbanked populations
Limited online information available about specific interest rates, fees, minimum balances, or loan terms
best_for: Atlanta-area residents seeking community banking with local branch access and member-owned governance
Borrowers needing real estate loans or mortgage financing from a community-focused lender
Credit union members valuing personal relationships and local decision-making over large corporate banking
diagnosis: B.O.N.D. Community Federal Credit Union is best for Atlanta residents who prefer community banking with local branch service and member-owned governance. The main caveat is its limited geographic reach (2 branches only) and smaller product range compared to larger financial institutions, so prospective members should confirm specific loan rates, fees, and eligibility requirements directly before opening accounts.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
